trade.summary: Italy is one of the UK's most important EU trading partners post-Brexit. The UK imports Italian food and beverages, machinery, luxury goods, and clothing. The UK exports machinery, financial services, and pharmaceuticals to Italy. Brexit created trade friction especially for agri-food and creative industries.
disputes: post-Brexit trade frictions in food and agri-food
UK-EU fishing rights affecting both sides

diplomatic.summary: The UK and Italy have navigated the post-Brexit relationship pragmatically. The Meloni government has generally maintained constructive UK-Italy relations, with both governments sharing some conservative political DNA. Both countries align on NATO and Ukraine support. The GCAP (Global Combat Air Programme) — a joint UK-Italy-Japan next-generation fighter — is a major bilateral defence initiative that underpins the relationship.

military.summary: Strong defence cooperation. The GCAP (Global Combat Air Programme) is a flagship UK-Italy-Japan joint programme for next-generation fighter jets. Both are NATO allies with closely aligned threat assessments on Russia. UK and Italian navies cooperate in Mediterranean operations. Joint participation in NATO enhanced forward presence.

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment. UK financial services are important for Italian companies accessing capital markets. Italian luxury brands (Ferrari, Maserati, Prada) have large UK operations. UK companies invest in Italian manufacturing and real estate. Post-Brexit, some Italian investment shifted to continental EU hubs.

energy.summary: UK and Italy both import natural gas and are working to diversify away from Russian supplies. Italy's pivotal role in Mediterranean energy infrastructure (trans-Mediterranean pipelines) indirectly affects UK energy security. Limited direct bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: Strong research ties through shared participation in CERN, ESA, and bilateral agreements. Many Italian researchers work in UK universities and research centres. Joint projects in physics, life sciences, and climate. Brexit created some barriers (Erasmus departure) but Horizon Europe re-association restored research ties.
